title: Static Accessibility Heuristics, Semantics, and Human Review · Transfer Build
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers static accessibility heuristics, semantics, and human review into a changed WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using context and route uncertain cases to review.
Audit page language, heading outline evidence, landmark purpose, labels, table relationships, and duplicate identifiers without enforcing cosmetic templates.
Identify native control and relationship evidence while acknowledging accessible names and states can depend on computed browser semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Image Alt Context
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 6933 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to map static image, structure, language, semantics, and interaction risks to WCAG with review handoff; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Image Alt Context.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify informative, decorative, functional, linked, complex, grouped, and text images using context and route uncertain cases to review. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Image Alt Context, keep Image Alt Context connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Conformance Nonclaim
steps.instruction:

Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

options.text: Keep the plausible Conformance Nonclaim output and hide the violated WCAG-linked static audit with explicit limits assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Conformance Nonclaim.
